practicals.title: Practical 1: Study of MySQL Open Source Software
practicals.desc: Study MySQL open source software and discuss its characteristics such as efficiency, scalability, performance, and transactional properties.

practicals.title: Practical 2: Study of SQLite
practicals.desc: Study SQLite, its uses, and comparison with SQL database systems.

practicals.title: Practical 3: Database Design and ER Diagram
practicals.desc: Design a database with at least two entities and relationships. Draw suitable ER/EER diagram for the system.

practicals.title: Practical 4: Database Implementation using DDL and DML
practicals.desc: Implement database using DDL and DML statements. Create tables with primary key, foreign key, and constraints.

practicals.title: Practical 5: SQL Operations
practicals.desc: Perform operations such as ALTER, DROP, INDEX, relational operators, pattern matching, aggregate functions with GROUP BY and HAVING clauses.

practicals.title: Practical 6: Advanced SQL Queries
practicals.desc: Perform nested queries, set operators, views, and sorting operations in SQL.

practicals.title: Practical 7: PL/SQL Procedures and Functions
practicals.desc: Write and execute PL/SQL stored procedures and functions to perform database operations and demonstrate their usage.

practicals.title: Practical 8: Database Triggers
practicals.desc: Write and execute database triggers to maintain data integrity and automate database actions.
